question_text: Two electromagnets, A and B, are made from identical wire coils and iron cores. Electromagnet A uses a 6V battery, and Electromagnet B uses a 12V battery. Both are switched on for the same time period. Which electromagnet will be stronger, and explain why in terms of current and magnetic field?
correct_answer: Electromagnet B will be stronger. A higher voltage (12V) drives a larger current through the coil compared to 6V. A larger current creates a stronger magnetic field around the iron core, which causes greater induced magnetism in the iron. Therefore, electromagnet B will pick up more magnetic objects.
explanation: Higher voltage makes more current flow through the wire, and more current creates a stronger magnetic field in the electromagnet.
